words.definition_vi: được cho là, theo lời khai (thường dùng khi chưa xác nhận hoàn toàn, mang tính hoài nghi)
words.definition_en: claimed or asserted to be true, but not proven; supposedly
words.example_sentence: The suspect allegedly committed the crime, but the evidence is still being investigated by authorities.

words.definition_vi: đáng ngờ, không chắc chắn, mang tính hoài nghi (thường có sắc thái tiêu cực)
words.definition_en: not clearly true or honest; questionable in quality or authenticity
words.example_sentence: The company's claims about the product's effectiveness are dubious and lack scientific support.

words.definition_vi: đáng khen, xứng đáng được ca ngợi (sắc thái tích cực, phê phán tốt)
words.definition_en: deserving praise and approval; worthy of commendation
words.example_sentence: Her commendable efforts to improve student literacy rates have earned recognition from the education ministry.

words.definition_vi: ít ỏi, không đủ, thiếu thốn (mang sắc thái tiêu cực, chỉ sự thiếu hụt)
words.definition_en: small in quantity and not enough; scanty or inadequate
words.example_sentence: The refugee camp provided only meager supplies of food and clean water to thousands of displaced families.

words.definition_vi: thận trọng, sáng suốt, cẩn trọng (sắc thái tích cực, chỉ sự khôn ngoan)
words.definition_en: careful and sensible in making decisions; showing good judgment
words.example_sentence: It is prudent to save a portion of your income for emergencies rather than spending everything immediately.

words.definition_vi: liều lĩnh, bất cẩn, không quan tâm hậu quả (sắc thái tiêu cực, chỉ sự vô trách nhiệm)
words.definition_en: without care or attention to danger or consequences; careless and irresponsible
words.example_sentence: The driver's reckless behavior on the highway endangered the lives of other motorists and passengers.

words.definition_vi: hình như, thoạt nhìn (mang sắc thái hoài nghi, chỉ điều không phải như vẻ ngoài)
words.definition_en: apparently or seemingly; as appears to be true on the surface
words.example_sentence: The government ostensibly supports environmental protection, yet continues to approve industrial projects that harm ecosystems.

words.definition_vi: gay gắt, chỉ trích gay gắt (sắc thái tiêu cực, chỉ lời phê bình cực kỳ khắc nghiệt)
words.definition_en: severely critical or harshly condemning; bitterly sarcastic
words.example_sentence: The film critic delivered a scathing review of the movie, calling it a waste of time and talent.

words.definition_vi: lành tính, không gây hại, tốt bụng (sắc thái tích cực, chỉ điều vô hại hoặc tốt)
words.definition_en: not harmful or dangerous; gentle and kind in nature
words.example_sentence: The doctor assured the patient that the tumor was benign and would not require aggressive treatment.

words.definition_vi: tầm thường, vô ích, không nghiêm túc (sắc thái tiêu cực, chỉ sự thiếu trọng yếu)
words.definition_en: not serious or important; trivial and lacking in seriousness
words.example_sentence: The judge dismissed the frivolous lawsuit, stating that it had no legal merit whatsoever.

words.definition_vi: minh oan, chứng minh là đúng (sắc thái tích cực, chỉ sự xóa bỏ nghi ngờ)
words.definition_en: to clear someone of blame or suspicion; to prove or justify something to be correct
words.example_sentence: The new evidence vindicated the defendant, proving his innocence after years of wrongful imprisonment.

words.definition_vi: mơ hồ, không rõ ràng, có thể hiểu theo nhiều cách (trung lập hoặc tiêu cực tùy ngữ cảnh)
words.definition_en: open to more than one interpretation; unclear or uncertain in meaning
words.example_sentence: The politician's ambiguous statement left voters confused about his actual position on the healthcare reform.

words.definition_vi: thẳng thắn, chân thành, không giấu giếm (sắc thái tích cực, chỉ sự trung thực)
words.definition_en: frank, honest, and straightforward; expressing opinions freely without reservation
words.example_sentence: In her candid interview, the actress discussed her struggles with mental health and addiction openly.

words.definition_vi: lừa dối, gây hiểu lầm (sắc thái tiêu cực, chỉ sự không trung thực)
words.definition_en: misleading or intended to trick; giving a false impression
words.example_sentence: The advertisement used deceptive marketing tactics to convince consumers that the product was more effective than it actually was.

words.definition_vi: hùng biện, lôi cuốn, diễn đạt tốt (sắc thái tích cực, chỉ sự biểu đạt xuất sắc)
words.definition_en: fluent, persuasive, and expressive in speaking or writing
words.example_sentence: The speaker's eloquent speech moved the audience to tears and inspired them to take action.

words.definition_vi: tầm thường, nhàm chán, bình thường (sắc thái tiêu cực hoặc trung lập, chỉ sự thiếu hứng thú)
words.definition_en: ordinary, dull, and lacking excitement or interest
words.example_sentence: Despite the mundane setting of the small town, the author created a fascinating story about its hidden secrets.

words.definition_vi: nghiêm ngặt, khắt khe, chặt chẽ (sắc thái trung lập hoặc tích cực, chỉ sự yêu cầu cao)
words.definition_en: strict, severe, or demanding; requiring strict attention to detail
words.example_sentence: The university has stringent academic standards that require students to maintain a high GPA to remain enrolled.

words.definition_vi: bề ngoài, nông cạn, không sâu sắc (sắc thái tiêu cực, chỉ sự thiếu chiều sâu)
words.definition_en: existing or occurring only on the surface; lacking depth or substance
words.example_sentence: The critic argued that the novel offered only a superficial treatment of complex social issues.

words.definition_vi: thực tế, thực dụng, dựa trên thực tế (sắc thái tích cực, chỉ sự cân nhắc thực tế)
words.definition_en: dealing with things in a practical, realistic way based on actual circumstances
words.example_sentence: The government took a pragmatic approach to the housing crisis by implementing affordable housing programs.

words.definition_vi: báo thù, tính trả thù (sắc thái tiêu cực, chỉ sự muốn trả thù)
words.definition_en: seeking to harm someone in return for perceived injury; vengeful
words.example_sentence: The vindictive former employee spread false rumors about her ex-boss to damage his professional reputation.

words.definition_vi: công bằng, không thiên vị (sắc thái tích cực, chỉ sự trung lập và công bằng)
words.definition_en: treating all sides or parties equally; not biased or prejudiced
words.example_sentence: The judge must remain impartial throughout the trial to ensure a fair verdict for both the prosecution and defense.

words.definition_vi: mờ ám, không rõ ràng, khó hiểu (sắc thái tiêu cực hoặc trung lập, chỉ sự thiếu sáng tỏ)
words.definition_en: not clearly expressed or easily understood; vague or unclear
words.example_sentence: The author's obscure writing style made it difficult for readers to understand the main themes of the novel.

words.definition_vi: tỉ mỉ, cẩn thận, chính xác (sắc thái tích cực, chỉ sự chú ý chi tiết)
words.definition_en: showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise
words.example_sentence: The researcher's meticulous documentation of the experiment ensured that other scientists could replicate the results.

words.definition_vi: hoài nghi, bi quan, không tin tưởng (sắc thái tiêu cực, chỉ sự mất niềm tin)
words.definition_en: believing that people are motivated by self-interest; distrusting human sincerity
words.example_sentence: The cynical journalist questioned the politician's motives, suspecting that his charitable donations were merely for publicity.

questions.question_text: The government's statement about the environmental impact was _____, leaving citizens unsure whether the project would actually harm the ecosystem.
questions.explanation: Ambiguous (mơ hồ, không rõ ràng) phù hợp với ngữ cảnh 'leaving citizens unsure' - chỉ sự không rõ ràng. Candid có nghĩa thẳng thắn (trái ngược), meticulous là tỉ mỉ, pragmatic là thực tế.

questions.question_text: The company's marketing campaign was _____ in claiming that their product could cure diseases without any scientific evidence to support it.
questions.explanation: Deceptive (lừa dối, gây hiểu lầm) phù hợp với ngữ cảnh 'without scientific evidence' - chỉ sự không trung thực. Prudent là thận trọng, eloquent là hùng biện, stringent là nghiêm ngặt.

questions.question_text: The journalist's _____ investigation revealed corruption at the highest levels of government, earning her international recognition.
questions.explanation: Meticulous (tỉ mỉ, cẩn thận) phù hợp với ngữ cảnh 'revealed corruption' - chỉ sự điều tra kỹ lưỡng. Superficial là bề ngoài, frivolous là tầm thường, mundane là nhàm chán.

questions.question_text: The witness's _____ account of the accident was crucial to the court's decision, as she described exactly what she saw without exaggeration.
questions.explanation: Candid (thẳng thắn, chân thành) phù hợp với 'described exactly what she saw without exaggeration'. Dubious là đáng ngờ, deceptive là lừa dối, ostensibly là hình như.

questions.question_text: The critic's _____ review of the film was so harsh that it discouraged many people from watching it.
questions.explanation: Scathing (gay gắt, chỉ trích khắc nghiệt) phù hợp với 'so harsh that it discouraged'. Commendable là đáng khen, benign là lành tính, pragmatic là thực tế.

questions.question_text: The defendant _____ committed the crime, but the prosecution has not yet presented sufficient evidence to prove guilt beyond reasonable doubt.
questions.explanation: Allegedly (được cho là, theo lời khai) phù hợp với ngữ cảnh 'but the prosecution has not yet presented sufficient evidence' - chỉ sự chưa xác nhận. Eloquently là hùng biện, pragmatically là thực tế, metriculously là tỉ mỉ.

questions.question_text: Which word is closest in meaning to 'prudent' in the context of financial planning?
questions.explanation: Cautious (cẩn thận) là từ đồng nghĩa gần nhất với prudent (thận trọng, sáng suốt). Reckless là liều lĩnh (trái ngược), frivolous là tầm thường, vindictive là báo thù.

questions.question_text: The _____ claims made by the company about its product's benefits were later proven to be false by independent researchers.
questions.explanation: Dubious (đáng ngờ, không chắc chắn) phù hợp với 'later proven to be false' - chỉ sự không đáng tin. Commendable là đáng khen, impartial là công bằng, eloquent là hùng biện.

questions.question_text: Which word best describes someone who holds grudges and seeks revenge against those who have wronged them?
questions.explanation: Vindictive (báo thù, tính trả thù) mô tả người muốn trả thù. Pragmatic là thực tế, benign là lành tính, impartial là công bằng.

questions.question_text: The professor's _____ explanation of the complex theory helped students understand the subject matter more clearly.
questions.explanation: Eloquent (hùng biện, lôi cuốn) phù hợp với 'helped students understand more clearly'. Obscure là mờ ám, superficial là bề ngoài, cynical là hoài nghi.

questions.question_text: Which word is most similar in meaning to 'unbiased' when discussing a fair decision-maker?
questions.explanation: Impartial (công bằng, không thiên vị) là từ đồng nghĩa với 'unbiased'. Cynical là hoài nghi, stringent là nghiêm ngặt, meager là ít ỏi.

questions.question_text: The _____ approach taken by the government to address the housing shortage proved effective, as it focused on practical solutions rather than idealistic theories.
questions.explanation: Pragmatic (thực tế, thực dụng) phù hợp với 'practical solutions rather than idealistic theories'. Vindictive là báo thù, superficial là bề ngoài, cynical là hoài nghi.
